config_file_locations.windows_note: Blizzard installs WoW to Program Files by default. Config.wtf uses a SET key value format (not INI sections). The Battle.net launcher rewrites some keys; edits made while the game is running may be overwritten. Edit only while WoW is closed.

keys.notes: WoW streams textures progressively. Ultra+ (5) requires 8 GB+ VRAM to prevent constant streaming stutter in densely populated zones such as Orgrimmar and Valdrakken.
Critical for raid performance. At Ultra in a 25-player raid with heavy AoE, GPU and CPU frame time can double versus Low. Recommend Low/Fair for progression raiders on mid-tier hardware.

keys.effect: Water and liquid surface rendering quality: 0=low, 1=fair, 2=good, 3=high, 4=ultra.

keys.notes: One of the highest CPU-cost settings in open-world zones. Reducing from Ultra to High in zones like The War Within's underground areas recovers meaningful CPU frame time.

keys.effect: Ground clutter (grass, small foliage) density and draw distance: 0=low, 1=fair, 2=good, 3=high, 4=ultra.
Maximum frame rate cap when the game window is focused.
keys.notes: WoW's default is uncapped. Setting a cap 10 above monitor refresh reduces GPU heat output without impacting perceived smoothness.

keys.effect: Maximum frame rate when the game window is in the background (unfocused).
keys.notes: Default is uncapped. Capping background FPS to 30 dramatically reduces GPU/CPU usage when the game is minimised during loading screens or AFK situations.
